Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement multi-hop reasoning in the routing loop

Transform the routing loop from simple retrieval to genuine multi-hop
reasoning. Each iteration now re-queries the FAISS index with the
evolving combined state of working memory, discovering knowledge that
was unreachable from the original query alone.

Key changes:
- Step 1 (Multi-hop discovery): re-query index with combined state,
  exclude already-seen columns, max 3 new per hop
- Step 3 (Competition): blended similarity (60% query + 40% context)
  so multi-hop discoveries survive while staying on-topic
- Step 7 (Convergence): converge when no new knowledge is discoverable
  AND state is stable, not when state matches query
- RoutingResult.reasoning_trace tracks what was discovered at each hop
- ColumnStore.find_relevant accepts exclude_ids for multi-hop filtering

Tested: 4-hop chain for "How do living things get energy from the sun?"
chains through photosynthesis -> oxygen -> blood -> circulatory system.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: session expiry, schedule refresh, and data type safety (v1.1.0)

Fix three bugs discovered after initial deployment:

1. Stale data after long uptime — the HTTP session (CSRF token + cookies)
   was never refreshed after the initial connection. Added SESSION_MAX_AGE
   (1 hour) with automatic session refresh, so schedule data stays current.

2. Schedule data lost after session refresh — the coordinator only read
   schedule data from the client once (on first load). After session refresh,
   new HTML-parsed schedule data was ignored. Introduced a dirty-flag pattern:
   _schedule_dirty is set when HTML is parsed, and the coordinator consumes
   it via consume_schedule_update() on every polling cycle.

3. Crash on unexpected API data format — DTEK API sometimes returns a list
   instead of a dict for fact/preset schedule data, causing
   "AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'items'". Added isinstance
   checks in _apply_schedule() and _get_day_slots() with graceful fallback.

commit.message:
fix: /amphion release resolution — replace curl-sI with Python+curl API call

Shell variables (RELEASE_TAG, DOWNLOAD_URL) do not persist across
separate Bash tool invocations in Claude Code. The previous multi-step
approach silently fell back to main branch on every run.

Replace both the fresh-install Step 1 and Environment Update steps 1-3
with single Python heredoc scripts that use subprocess.run(["curl",...])
to call the GitHub API, resolve the latest release tag, and download —
all in one atomic execution. Uses curl instead of urllib to avoid macOS
SSL certificate verification failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: EGL-Shim via LD_PRELOAD – AMD-Absturz auf Fedora/Bazzite beheben

Ursache: amdgpu_device_initialize schlägt mit EACCES (-13) fehl →
eglGetDisplay(EGL_DEFAULT_DISPLAY) = EGL_NO_DISPLAY → webkit2gtk 2.44
ruft g_error() → WebKitWebProcess-Absturz (SIGABRT).

Das AppRun-Skript überschreibt LD_PRELOAD, daher wird der Shim im
Rust-Prozess selbst gesetzt (nach AppRun, vor app_lib::run()) – WebKit-
WebProcess erbt es als Kindprozess.

- src-tauri/egl_shim/egl_shim.c: fängt eglGetDisplay() ab, gibt Sentinel
  statt NULL zurück → verhindert g_error(); eglInitialize() mit Sentinel
  gibt EGL_FALSE zurück → graceful Fallback auf Software-Compositing
- src-tauri/src/main.rs: setzt LD_PRELOAD=$APPDIR/usr/lib/egl_shim.so
  wenn im AppImage-Kontext (APPDIR gesetzt)
- build.yml + test-appimage.yml: Shim kompilieren + ins AppImage einbetten

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

B1 fix: surface static r-confidence; discard model confidence head

The model confidence head is anti-calibrated (outputs constants
regardless of input). calibration.json already discards it via
scale=0.0 in the linear confidence calibration — this commit makes
that explicit and propagates the static r-value to the API response.

Changes:
- student.js: add getDimensionRConfidence() method; add r_confidence
  field to per-dimension score output; document B1 behavior in
  calibrateConfidence() JSDoc
- server.js: populate calibration_note per dimension with held-out r
  value; update anti-calibration-confidence limitation to reflect
  actual behavior (confidence = static r, not per-prediction)

commit.message:
fix: move useMemo hooks before early return to fix hook order violation

React hooks must always be called in the same order. The useMemo calls
were placed after the `if (!stats) return` early exit, causing a React
error #310 (invalid hook call) on first render.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make deploy stage non-blocking to decouple from pipeline loop (#74)

runDeployStage no longer calls WaitIdle — it launches the session and
returns immediately. The next check-in cycle monitors the session via
advanceDeploy's session status check. This decouples deploy pipelines
from implementation pipelines so they can run concurrently.

Co-authored-by: lucas <lucas@MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Period start is now 1st of next month, next_billing_date always lands on end of month

- Right-align totals labels to prevent overlap with large amounts
- Widen column spacing to fix Einzelpreis header overlap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate variable SCPI latency from NTP SHM0 offset

The SCPI TIME:STRing? query completes 200–500 ms after the GPS second
boundary. Writing recv_time as SHM0 receiveTimeStamp therefore reported
a variable negative offset (= −SCPI latency) to NTP, causing the GPS
source to appear jittery and eventually be flagged as a falseticker.

Two-part fix:
1. TOD mode (normal): SHM0 is now updated from the TOD thread together
   with SHM1. The TOD packet arrives ~50 ms after the PPS edge, so the
   offset is small and stable – NTP can learn it as a fixed correction.
   The polling loop no longer writes SHM0 when TOD is active.

2. Polling-only mode (no TOD): SHM0 uses gps_time as both clockTime and
   receiveTime (offset = 0). This is honest for a 1-second precision
   source and avoids reporting the random SCPI round-trip delay as a
   clock offset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

session: remove wakeup channel, use ticker-only mail processing

The wakeup channel caused duplicate message delivery: inbox delivery
and idle state transitions both signaled an immediate wakeup with
bypassIdleCheck=true, prompting bots before they were ready.

Replace with a simple 5-second ticker. The idle threshold is always
applied, eliminating the race condition. Worst-case delivery latency
is 10 seconds (one tick to drain outboxes, next tick to prompt).

Address all findings from architecture review:

- P1-01: Ctrl+C without selection now routes through executeCommandByName("Quit")
  so unsaved changes prompt is shown (was bypassing save prompt)
- P1-02: Remove all TODO/FIXME markers (11 → 0) — stale comments removed,
  genuine gaps converted to descriptive notes
- P1-03: Remove unused AlignedModel cache (3 fields, computeCacheKey,
  InvalidateAlignedCache) — cache was invalidated every keypress and
  unreachable from View() due to value receiver
- P2-01: Remove dead lineWrap field (declared, never read)
- P2-02: Extract autocomplete handler from model.go (1119 → 775 LOC)
- P2-03: Add command name consistency test to catch string dispatch typos
- P2-04: Add "New" command to help overlay for discoverability
- P2-05: Surface swallowed errors in newFile() and transitionToReady()

Also fix modernize warnings in cmd/doceval (CutPrefix, SplitSeq).

- Remove unnecessary OSFT guard for direct VLMs (patterns match fine)

  align_model_and_tokenizer (vocab_size, pad/bos/eos_token_id)

commit.message:
First boot: game executes through kernel init and heap alloc

- Fix XBE section raw offsets (were Burnout3 values, now Wreckless)
- Fix SEH prolog/epilog addresses for Wreckless (0xF0954/0xF098D)
- Add manually identified functions: thread entry (0xE8A42), game
  init (0xEB50F) to disassembler function list
- Wire up actual entry point call (xbe_entry_point)
- Add register state dump on crash for debugging
- Switch to console subsystem for stdout capture
- Remove duplicate global register definitions (use kernel lib's)
- Game now boots: creates thread, makes 10 kernel calls, allocates
  heap before crashing on uninitialized pointer dereference

commit.message:
[feature] Model fallback chain — auto-switch on rate limit exhaustion

When primary model (e.g. gemini-3.1-flash-lite) exhausts all 10 retries,
automatically falls back to gemini-2.5-flash, then gemini-2.0-flash-lite.
Each fallback gets one attempt before trying the next in the chain.

Settings — full env dashboard, 35 services across 8 categories

General page rebuilt as Environment Dashboard:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(il,zia): IL optimizer improvements and lowering optimizations

Tier 1 — optimizer passes:
- EarlyCSE: add fixpoint iteration (max 4 passes) for multi-pass CSE
- LICM: refine load hoisting to allow non-escaping alloca loads past
  modifying calls via new BasicAA::isNonEscapingAlloca() accessor
- ValueKey: add ConstStr and GAddr to safe CSE opcodes, eliminating
  redundant address materializations in EarlyCSE/GVN
- Verifier: replace reachability check with full Cooper-Harvey-Kennedy
  dominator computation for proper dominance verification of SSA uses

Tier 2 — lowering and new pass:
- Virtual dispatch: replace O(N) if-else chain with SwitchI32 for
  multi-implementation method dispatch in Lowerer_Dispatch.cpp
- Match expressions: emit SwitchI32 fast path for integer-only match
  arms without guards, falling back to generic lowering otherwise
- Reassociation: new pass canonicalizing operand order for
  commutative+associative integer ops (Add/Mul/And/Or/Xor), placed
  before EarlyCSE in the O2 pipeline for better CSE opportunities

All 1267 tests pass. All 11 demos build successfully.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Startup Idea Radar at /ideas

New page that scans live trend signals and uses Claude Opus to generate
actionable $1B startup ideas:

Backend:
- GET /api/trends — fetches GitHub trending repos, HN top stories, and
  arXiv AI papers in parallel via httpx; returns normalised TrendItem list
- POST /api/spark-ideas — accepts selected TrendItems, builds structured
  Claude Opus prompt, returns 5 startup ideas with problem/solution/
  why_now/market/revenue/inspiration fields
- New Pydantic models: TrendItem, TrendsResponse, SparkIdeasRequest,
  SparkIdea, SparkIdeasResponse

Frontend (frontend/app/ideas/):
- IdeasClient: trend grid with source filter tabs (GitHub/HN/AI Research),
  selectable cards, skeleton loading, "Spark Ideas" CTA with Claude Opus
  analysis, generated idea cards with expand/collapse for details

  the full 8-agent studio session
